message.content:
Explore the current state of the Turborepo monorepo at C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden. This was created with `npx create-turbo@latest`. I need to understand:

1. Full directory structure (all files and folders)
2. Root package.json - scripts, dependencies, workspaces config
3. turbo.json - current task pipeline config
4. All apps/ contents - what apps exist, their package.json, main entry files
5. All packages/ contents - what packages exist, their package.json, configs
6. Any tsconfig files at root and in workspaces
7. .gitignore contents

Be very thorough - list every file path you find. I need to know exactly what exists to plan what to keep, modify, and delete.

content.text: I'll thoroughly explore the Turborepo monorepo structure. Let me start with a comprehensive scan of all files and directories.

content.content: <system-reminder>Warning: the file exists but is shorter than the provided offset (1). The file has 1 lines.</system-reminder>

1→# Turborepo starter
     2→
     3→This Turborepo starter is maintained by the Turborepo core team.
     4→
     5→## Using this example
     6→
     7→Run the following command:
     8→
     9→```sh
    10→npx create-turbo@latest
    11→```
    12→
    13→## What's inside?
    14→
    15→This Turborepo includes the following packages/apps:
    16→
    17→### Apps and Packages
    18→
    19→- `docs`: a [Next.js](https://nextjs.org/) app
    20→- `web`: another [Next.js](https://nextjs.org/) app
    21→- `@repo/ui`: a stub React component library shared by both `web` and `docs` applications
    22→- `@repo/eslint-config`: `eslint` configurations (includes `eslint-config-next` and `eslint-config-prettier`)
    23→- `@repo/typescript-config`: `tsconfig.json`s used throughout the monorepo
    24→
    25→Each package/app is 100% [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/).
    26→
    27→### Utilities
    28→
    29→This Turborepo has some additional tools already setup for you:
    30→
    31→- [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) for static type checking
    32→- [ESLint](https://eslint.org/) for code linting
    33→- [Prettier](https://prettier.io) for code formatting
    34→
    35→### Build
    36→
    37→To build all apps and packages, run the following command:
    38→
    39→With [global `turbo`](https://turborepo.dev/docs/getting-started/installation#global-installation) installed (recommended):
    40→
    41→```sh
    42→cd my-turborepo
    43→turbo build
    44→```
    45→
    46→Without global `turbo`, use your package manager:
    47→
    48→```sh
    49→cd my-turborepo
    50→npx turbo build
    51→yarn dlx turbo build
    52→pnpm exec turbo build
    53→```
    54→
    55→You can build a specific package by using a [filter](https://turborepo.dev/docs/crafting-your-repository/running-tasks#using-filters):
    56→
    57→With [global `turbo`](https://turborepo.dev/docs/getting-started/installation#global-installation) installed:
    58→
    59→```sh
    60→turbo build --filter=docs
    61→```
    62→
    63→Without global `turbo`:
    64→
    65→```sh
    66→npx turbo build --filter=docs
    67→yarn exec turbo build --filter=docs
    68→pnpm exec turbo build --filter=docs
    69→```
    70→
    71→### Develop
    72→
    73→To develop all apps and packages, run the following command:
    74→
    75→With [global `turbo`](https://turborepo.dev/docs/getting-started/installation#global-installation) installed (recommended):
    76→
    77→```sh
    78→cd my-turborepo
    79→turbo dev
    80→```
    81→
    82→Without global `turbo`, use your package manager:
    83→
    84→```sh
    85→cd my-turborepo
    86→npx turbo dev
    87→yarn exec turbo dev
    88→pnpm exec turbo dev
    89→```
    90→
    91→You can develop a specific package by using a [filter](https://turborepo.dev/docs/crafting-your-repository/running-tasks#using-filters):
    92→
    93→With [global `turbo`](https://turborepo.dev/docs/getting-started/installation#global-installation) installed:
    94→
    95→```sh
    96→turbo dev --filter=web
    97→```
    98→
    99→Without global `turbo`:
   100→
   101→```sh
   102→npx turbo dev --filter=web
   103→yarn exec turbo dev --filter=web
   104→pnpm exec turbo dev --filter=web
   105→```
   106→
   107→### Remote Caching
   108→
   109→> [!TIP]
   110→> Vercel Remote Cache is free for all plans. Get started today at [vercel.com](https://vercel.com/signup?utm_source=remote-cache-sdk&utm_campaign=free_remote_cache).
   111→
   112→Turborepo can use a technique known as [Remote Caching](https://turborepo.dev/docs/core-concepts/remote-caching) to share cache artifacts across machines, enabling you to share build caches with your team and CI/CD pipelines.
   113→
   114→By default, Turborepo will cache locally. To enable Remote Caching you will need an account with Vercel. If you don't have an account you can [create one](https://vercel.com/signup?utm_source=turborepo-examples), then enter the following commands:
   115→
   116→With [global `turbo`](https://turborepo.dev/docs/getting-started/installation#global-installation) installed (recommended):
   117→
   118→```sh
   119→cd my-turborepo
   120→turbo login
   121→```
   122→
   123→Without global `turbo`, use your package manager:
   124→
   125→```sh
   126→cd my-turborepo
   127→npx turbo login
   128→yarn exec turbo login
   129→pnpm exec turbo login
   130→```
   131→
   132→This will authenticate the Turborepo CLI with your [Vercel account](https://vercel.com/docs/concepts/personal-accounts/overview).
   133→
   134→Next, you can link your Turborepo to your Remote Cache by running the following command from the root of your Turborepo:
   135→
   136→With [global `turbo`](https://turborepo.dev/docs/getting-started/installation#global-installation) installed:
   137→
   138→```sh
   139→turbo link
   140→```
   141→
   142→Without global `turbo`:
   143→
   144→```sh
   145→npx turbo link
   146→yarn exec turbo link
   147→pnpm exec turbo link
   148→```
   149→
   150→## Useful Links
   151→
   152→Learn more about the power of Turborepo:
   153→
   154→- [Tasks](https://turborepo.dev/docs/crafting-your-repository/running-tasks)
   155→- [Caching](https://turborepo.dev/docs/crafting-your-repository/caching)
   156→- [Remote Caching](https://turborepo.dev/docs/core-concepts/remote-caching)
   157→- [Filtering](https://turborepo.dev/docs/crafting-your-repository/running-tasks#using-filters)
   158→- [Configuration Options](https://turborepo.dev/docs/reference/configuration)
   159→- [CLI Usage](https://turborepo.dev/docs/reference/command-line-reference)
   160→

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding of the project. Let me present a comprehensive report:

## Complete Turborepo Monorepo Structure Analysis

### Root Directory Files

```
C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\
├── .gitignore
├── .npmrc (empty)
├── .claude/
│   └── settings.local.json
├── .git/ (git repository)
├── CLAUDE.md
├── README.md
├── package.json
├── package-lock.json
├── turbo.json
├── apps/
└── packages/
```

---

### 1. Root Configuration Files

#### **package.json** - Root Workspace Configuration
- **Type**: Root workspace config (npm v11.6.2+, Node >=18)
- **Scripts**:
  - `dev`: `turbo run dev`
  - `build`: `turbo run build`
  - `lint`: `turbo run lint`
  - `format`: Prettier for `**/*.{ts,tsx,md}`
  - `check-types`: `turbo run check-types`
- **Workspaces**: `apps/*` and `packages/*`
- **Root DevDeps**:
  - `prettier` ^3.7.4
  - `turbo` ^2.8.20
  - `typescript` 5.9.2

#### **turbo.json** - Task Pipeline Configuration
```json
{
  "ui": "tui",
  "tasks": {
    "build": { "dependsOn": ["^build"], "outputs": [".next/**", "!.next/cache/**"] },
    "lint": { "dependsOn": ["^lint"] },
    "check-types": { "dependsOn": ["^check-types"] },
    "dev": { "cache": false, "persistent": true }
  }
}
```

#### **.gitignore** - Standard Next.js monorepo ignores
- Dependencies: `node_modules`, `.pnp`
- Local env files: `.env*`
- Turbo cache: `.turbo`
- Build outputs: `.next/`, `out/`, `build`, `dist`
- Testing: `coverage`
- Vercel: `.vercel`
- Debug logs: npm/yarn error logs
- Misc: `.DS_Store`, `*.pem`

#### **.npmrc** - Empty npm config file

---

### 2. Apps Directory Structure

#### **apps/web/** - Main Next.js Application (Port 3000)
```
apps/web/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── next.config.js
├── eslint.config.js
├── next-env.d.ts
├── app/
│   ├── layout.tsx
│   ├── page.tsx
│   ├── page.module.css
│   ├── globals.css
│   └── fonts/
│       ├── GeistVF.woff
│       └── GeistMonoVF.woff
├── public/
│   ├── favicon.ico
│   ├── vercel.svg
│   ├── next.svg
│   ├── turborepo-dark.svg
│   ├── turborepo-light.svg
│   ├── globe.svg
│   ├── window.svg
│   └── file-text.svg
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
```

**package.json**:
- Type: `module`
- Scripts: `dev --port 3000`, `build`, `start`, `lint`, `check-types`
- Dependencies: `@repo/ui` (workspace), `next` 16.2.0, `react` ^19.2.0, `react-dom` ^19.2.0
- DevDeps: `@repo/eslint-config`, `@repo/typescript-config`, TypeScript 5.9.2, ESLint 9.39.1

**tsconfig.json**:
- Extends: `@repo/typescript-config/nextjs.json`
- Adds Next.js plugin and `strictNullChecks: true`

#### **apps/docs/** - Documentation Next.js Application (Port 3001)
```
apps/docs/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── next.config.js
├── eslint.config.js
├── next-env.d.ts
├── app/
│   ├── layout.tsx
│   ├── page.tsx
│   ├── page.module.css
│   ├── globals.css
│   └── fonts/
├── public/
│   └── (same SVG files as web)
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
```

**Identical to web** except runs on port 3001.

---

### 3. Packages Directory Structure

#### **packages/ui/** - Shared React Component Library
```
packages/ui/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── eslint.config.mjs
├── src/
│   ├── button.tsx
│   ├── card.tsx
│   └── code.tsx
```

**package.json**:
- Uses **direct path exports**: `"./*": "./src/*.tsx"`
  - Components imported as: `import { Button } from "@repo/ui/button"`
  - No barrel exports (index.ts)
- Scripts: `lint`, `generate:component`, `check-types`
- All components are **client components** (`"use client"` directive)

**Component Files**:
1. **button.tsx** - Client component with `appName` prop, shows alert on click
2. **card.tsx** - Server-side component with title, children, href props
3. **code.tsx** - Simple code/pre wrapper component

**tsconfig.json**:
- Extends: `@repo/typescript-config/react-library.json`
- Adds `strictNullChecks: true`
- Output: `dist` directory

#### **packages/eslint-config/** - Shared ESLint Configurations
```
packages/eslint-config/
├── package.json
├── base.js
├── next.js
├── react-internal.js
├── README.md
```

**package.json**:
- Type: `module`
- Exports: `./base`, `./next-js`, `./react-internal`
- DevDeps: ESLint, TypeScript, Next, React plugins

**Configuration Files**:
1. **base.js** - Base ESLint config with JS, TypeScript, Turbo plugin, Prettier
2. **next.js** - Extends base, adds Next.js, React, React Hooks plugins
3. **react-internal.js** - Extends base, adds React and React Hooks (no Next.js)

#### **packages/typescript-config/** - Shared TypeScript Configurations
```
packages/typescript-config/
├── package.json
├── base.json
├── nextjs.json
├── react-library.json
```

**package.json**:
- No exports (configs used directly via extends)

**Configuration Files**:
1. **base.json** - Base config with:
   - `target`: ES2022
   - `module`: NodeNext
   - `moduleResolution`: NodeNext
   - `strict`: true
   - `lib`: ["es2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"]
   - `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`: true

2. **nextjs.json** - Extends base for Next.js:
   - `module`: ESNext
   - `moduleResolution`: Bundler
   - `allowJs`: true
   - `jsx`: preserve
   - `noEmit`: true

3. **react-library.json** - Extends base for React libraries:
   - `jsx`: react-jsx

---

### 4. Key Dependencies & Versions

| Package | Version | Use |
|---------|---------|-----|
| Next.js | 16.2.0 | Apps framework |
| React | 19.2.0 | UI library |
| TypeScript | 5.9.2 | Type checking |
| Turbo | 2.8.20 | Monorepo orchestration |
| ESLint | 9.39.1 | Linting |
| Prettier | 3.7.4 | Formatting |
| @types/react | 19.2.2 | React types |
| @types/node | 22.15.3 | Node types |

---

### 5. Complete File Listing by Category

#### Workspace Root Files
- `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\package.json`
- `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\turbo.json`
- `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\.gitignore`
- `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\.npmrc`
- `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\CLAUDE.md`
- `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\README.md`

#### Apps/Web Files (19 files)
- `apps/web/package.json`
- `apps/web/tsconfig.json`
- `apps/web/next.config.js`
- `apps/web/eslint.config.js`
- `apps/web/next-env.d.ts`
- `apps/web/.gitignore`
- `apps/web/README.md`
- `apps/web/app/layout.tsx`
- `apps/web/app/page.tsx`
- `apps/web/app/page.module.css`
- `apps/web/app/globals.css`
- `apps/web/app/fonts/GeistVF.woff`
- `apps/web/app/fonts/GeistMonoVF.woff`
- `apps/web/public/favicon.ico`
- `apps/web/public/vercel.svg`
- `apps/web/public/next.svg`
- `apps/web/public/turborepo-dark.svg`
- `apps/web/public/turborepo-light.svg`
- Plus 4 more SVG files in public/

#### Apps/Docs Files (19 files)
- `apps/docs/package.json`
- `apps/docs/tsconfig.json`
- `apps/docs/next.config.js`
- `apps/docs/eslint.config.js`
- `apps/docs/next-env.d.ts`
- `apps/docs/.gitignore`
- `apps/docs/README.md`
- `apps/docs/app/layout.tsx`
- `apps/docs/app/page.tsx`
- `apps/docs/app/page.module.css`
- `apps/docs/app/globals.css`
- `apps/docs/app/fonts/GeistVF.woff`
- `apps/docs/app/fonts/GeistMonoVF.woff`
- `apps/docs/public/` (same files as web)

#### Packages/UI Files (6 files)
- `packages/ui/package.json`
- `packages/ui/tsconfig.json`
- `packages/ui/eslint.config.mjs`
- `packages/ui/src/button.tsx`
- `packages/ui/src/card.tsx`
- `packages/ui/src/code.tsx`

#### Packages/ESLint-Config Files (5 files)
- `packages/eslint-config/package.json`
- `packages/eslint-config/base.js`
- `packages/eslint-config/next.js`
- `packages/eslint-config/react-internal.js`
- `packages/eslint-config/README.md`

#### Packages/TypeScript-Config Files (4 files)
- `packages/typescript-config/package.json`
- `packages/typescript-config/base.json`
- `packages/typescript-config/nextjs.json`
- `packages/typescript-config/react-library.json`

---

### 6. Summary Statistics

| Item | Count |
|------|-------|
| Total Apps | 2 |
| Total Packages | 3 |
| UI Components | 3 |
| TypeScript Configs | 3 |
| ESLint Configs | 3 |
| Total package.json files | 6 |
| Total tsconfig files | 5 |
| Total configuration files | 21 |

---

### 7. Architecture Overview

```
Monorepo Root (npm workspace)
├── apps/
│   ├── web (Next.js 16, port 3000) → depends on @repo/ui
│   └── docs (Next.js 16, port 3001) → depends on @repo/ui
└── packages/
    ├── @repo/ui (React component library, 3 components)
    ├── @repo/eslint-config (3 shareable configs)
    └── @repo/typescript-config (3 shareable configs)

Turbo Pipeline:
- build: depends on ^build, caches .next outputs
- lint: depends on ^lint
- check-types: depends on ^check-types
- dev: no cache, persistent
```

---

### 8. Key Points to Keep in Mind

1. **UI Package Export Pattern**: Uses direct path exports (`./*: ./src/*.tsx`) - no barrel index file
2. **Client vs Server Components**: 
   - UI `button.tsx` is client component (`"use client"`)
   - UI `card.tsx` and `code.tsx` are server components (default)
3. **Workspace Dependencies**: Apps use `@repo/*: "*"` (workspace protocol)
4. **TypeScript**: All workspaces use strict mode with `strictNullChecks: true`
5. **ESLint**: Modern flat config format (ESLint 9+)
6. **Package Manager**: npm v11.6.2+ enforced via engines field
7. **.claude directory**: Only has `settings.local.json` - ready for memory bank files

This is a clean, standard Turborepo scaffold created with `npx create-turbo@latest`, ready for customization.
